message:
feat(contract): AA-09.1 crash-only region semantics contract (45 tests)

Crash-only state machine (7 states), deterministic journal format
(8 entry types, 5 ordering rules), microreboot protocol (4 phases
with budget/backoff), cancellation interaction rules, and supervision
strategy mapping.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.9.1","title":"[SUPERSEDED] [AA-09.1] Define crash-only region semantics, journal format, and microreboot protocol","description":"Define the crash-only execution contract for regions and supervised components: what gets journaled, what gets abandoned, what must be replayable, and how microreboot interacts with cancellation and quiescence.","design":"Scope:\n- Treat crash and recovery as protocol phases, not implicit side effects.\n- Specify journal contents, ordering, and replay requirements.\n- Preserve capability and obligation boundaries through failure.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Crash-only state transitions are explicit and documented with journal semantics.\n2. Journal contents and ordering are deterministic and sufficient for replay/recovery.\n3. Unit tests protect journal serialization and crash-state transition logic.\n4. The design explains exactly how current shutdown/supervision semantics map onto the stronger crash-only mode.","notes":"Superseded by asupersync-1508v.9.4 after an interrupted shell pass created a duplicate task.  {"id":"asupersync-1508v.9.2","title":"[SUPERSEDED] [AA-09.2] Prototype failure-domain compiler, restart topology hooks, and recovery authority rules","description":"Prototype the machinery that turns crash-only semantics into runtime behavior: failure-domain boundaries, restart-topology compilation, authority revocation during recovery, and narrow recovery control seams.","design":"Scope:\n- Tie recovery behavior into supervision and capability rules.\n- Keep restart domains explicit and auditable.\n- Avoid hidden ambient authority during recovery.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Failure domains and restart topology choices are explicit and inspectable.\n2. Recovery-time authority is narrowed or revoked according to documented rules.\n3. Unit and E2E tests cover representative restart-domain behaviors and recovery authority transitions.\n4. Structured logs emit recovery domain IDs, restart decisions, and revocation actions.","notes":"Superseded by asupersync-1508v.9.5 after an interrupted shell pass created a duplicate task. Keep this issue only as audit history; the active canonical bead is asupersync-1508v.9.5.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:51:05.088399621Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T05:59:52.179440977Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T05:59:52.179334560Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa09-recovery","alien-artifact","capability","resilience","runtime-ascension"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.9.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:51:05.088399621Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.9.3","title":"[SUPERSEDED] [AA-09.3] Run soak, fault-injection, replay, and MTTR validation for crash-only mode","description":"Explicit validation bead for the recovery track: soak tests, injected crashes, replay verification, and MTTR-style measurements that prove crash-only mode is bounded, inspectable, and worth enabling.","design":"Scope:\n- Exercise repeated crashes, partial recovery, stuck cleanup, and authority revocation under load.\n- Measure recovery latency and evidence quality.\n- Treat every failure as something that must be reproducible.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Deterministic unit and E2E suites cover crash/recovery scenarios, replay, and restart storms.\n2. Recovery metrics include MTTR-style measures, replay success, and invariant preservation.\n3. Structured logs and artifacts include crash IDs, recovery domain IDs, journal IDs, and replay commands.\n4. Any unreproducible recovery failure blocks graduation.","notes":"Superseded by asupersync-1508v.9.6 after an interrupted shell pass created a duplicate task.  {"id":"asupersync-151l5","title":"Epic: Actor System & Supervision (Phase 3)","description":"# Epic: Actor System & Supervision (Phase 3)

## Overview

This epic covers the complete implementation of the actor model with supervision trees, building on the region-based structured concurrency already in place. Actors are long-lived tasks owned by regions that can receive messages, maintain state, and be supervised for fault tolerance.

## Background & Context

The Asupersync roadmap defines Phase 3 as 'Actors + session types'. This builds directly on:
- **Phase 0-1**: Core types (Outcome, Budget, Cx), region tree, scheduler
- **Phase 2**: I/O reactor, timer driver, sync primitives, protocol stacks

Actors differ from regular tasks in several key ways:
1. **Long-lived**: Actors persist beyond single request/response cycles
2. **Stateful**: Actors encapsulate mutable state behind message-passing
3. **Supervised**: Parent actors can monitor and restart failed children
4. **Addressable**: Actors have stable identities for message routing

## Rationale

Why actors matter for Asupersync:
1. **Natural fit for protocols**: HTTP connections, gRPC streams, WebSocket sessions are all actor-shaped
2. **Fault isolation**: Actor boundaries contain failures, enabling graceful degradation
3. **Structured supervision**: Supervisor trees provide principled error recovery
4. **Type-safe communication**: Session types can prevent protocol violations at compile time

## Design Principles (from asupersync_plan_v4.md)

1. **Actors are region-owned**: An actor belongs to exactly one region; region close cancels the actor
2. **Mailboxes are bounded**: Backpressure via bounded channels prevents runaway memory usage
3. **Supervision is explicit**: Restart policies are declared, not discovered at runtime
4. **Session types optional**: Basic actors work without session types; session types add static guarantees

## Architecture

ActorSystem components:
- ActorRef<M>: Typed handle for sending messages
- ActorCell: Runtime state (mailbox, lifecycle, parent)
- Supervisor: Monitors children, applies restart policy
- RestartPolicy: OneForOne, OneForAll, RestForOne, custom
- SessionChannel<S>: Session-typed communication (optional)
- ActorContext: Cx extension with actor-specific capabilities

## Success Criteria

- Actors spawn and terminate correctly within regions
- Supervision restarts crashed actors according to policy
- Mailboxes enforce backpressure
- Actor cancellation follows the standard protocol (request→drain→finalize)
- Lab runtime can deterministically test actor interleavings
- Optional session types prevent protocol violations at compile time

## Dependencies

This epic BLOCKS on:
- bd-1j64: Runtime Core Maturity (need stable scheduling, I/O, timers)
- bd-3it3: Async sync primitives (actors use channels, locks internally)

## References

- asupersync_plan_v4.md Section 4.3: Execution Tiers (Actors)
- asupersync_plan_v4.md Section 5.2: Supervision and Restart
- asupersync_v4_formal_semantics.md: Actor semantics (planned)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-31T21:17:04.492874662Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T06:29:32.723841576Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T06:29:32.723766716Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["actors","phase3","supervision"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-151l5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1h8cp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:37Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-151l5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1p0xz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:37Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,244 @@
+{
+  "contract_version": "crash-only-region-semantics-v1",
+  "bead_id": "asupersync-1508v.9.4",
+  "description": "AA-09.1 crash-only region semantics, journal format, and microreboot protocol.",
+  "runner_script": "scripts/run_crash_only_region_smoke.sh",
+  "runner_bundle_schema_version": "crash-only-region-smoke-bundle-v1",
+  "runner_report_schema_version": "crash-only-region-smoke-run-report-v1",
+  "crash_state_machine": {
+    "description": "Explicit crash-only state transitions for supervised regions.",
+    "states": [
+      {
+        "state_id": "RUNNING",
+        "description": "Region is actively executing tasks and settling obligations.",
+        "transitions_to": ["CRASHING", "DRAINING"]
+      },
+      {
+        "state_id": "DRAINING",
+        "description": "Region is gracefully shutting down: no new tasks accepted, existing tasks run to completion.",
+        "transitions_to": ["QUIESCED", "CRASHING"]
+      },
+      {
+        "state_id": "CRASHING",
+        "description": "Region has encountered an unrecoverable fault. Journal checkpoint is written, obligations are abandoned.",
+        "transitions_to": ["JOURNALED"]
+      },
+      {
+        "state_id": "JOURNALED",
+        "description": "Crash journal has been durably written. Region state is frozen for replay.",
+        "transitions_to": ["RECOVERING", "TOMBSTONED"]
+      },
+      {
+        "state_id": "RECOVERING",
+        "description": "Microreboot in progress: journal is replayed, obligations are re-established.",
+        "transitions_to": ["RUNNING", "TOMBSTONED"]
+      },
+      {
+        "state_id": "QUIESCED",
+        "description": "Graceful drain complete. All obligations settled, region may be reaped.",
+        "transitions_to": ["TOMBSTONED", "RUNNING"]
+      },
+      {
+        "state_id": "TOMBSTONED",
+        "description": "Terminal state. Region resources are fully released and cannot be revived.",
+        "transitions_to": []
+      }
+    ],
+    "invariants": [
+      {
+        "invariant_id": "CSM-NO-SKIP",
+        "description": "No state transition may skip an intermediate state (e.g., RUNNING cannot go directly to RECOVERING)."
+      },
+      {
+        "invariant_id": "CSM-TOMBSTONE-TERMINAL",
+        "description": "TOMBSTONED is absorbing: no transitions out."
+      },
+      {
+        "invariant_id": "CSM-CRASH-ALWAYS-JOURNALS",
+        "description": "Every CRASHING state must transition to JOURNALED before any other state."
+      },
+      {
+        "invariant_id": "CSM-RECOVER-NEEDS-JOURNAL",
+        "description": "RECOVERING requires a valid JOURNALED predecessor."
+      }
+    ]
+  },
+  "journal_format": {
+    "description": "Deterministic journal for crash recovery and replay.",
+    "schema_version": "crash-journal-v1",
+    "entry_types": [
+      {
+        "entry_type": "JE-REGION-OPEN",
+        "description": "Region was opened with configuration.",
+        "required_fields": ["region_id", "parent_region_id", "supervision_strategy", "epoch", "timestamp"]
+      },
+      {
+        "entry_type": "JE-TASK-SPAWN",
+        "description": "Task was spawned in the region.",
+        "required_fields": ["task_id", "region_id", "task_name", "epoch", "sequence_number"]
+      },
+      {
+        "entry_type": "JE-OBLIGATION-ENTER",
+        "description": "Obligation was entered within a region.",
+        "required_fields": ["obligation_id", "region_id", "obligation_type", "epoch", "sequence_number"]
+      },
+      {
+        "entry_type": "JE-OBLIGATION-SETTLE",
+        "description": "Obligation was settled (fulfilled or abandoned).",
+        "required_fields": ["obligation_id", "region_id", "outcome", "epoch", "sequence_number"]
+      },
+      {
+        "entry_type": "JE-DECISION-APPLIED",
+        "description": "Controller decision was applied to the region.",
+        "required_fields": ["decision_id", "controller_id", "region_id", "decision_label", "epoch", "sequence_number"]
+      },
+      {
+        "entry_type": "JE-CHECKPOINT",
+        "description": "Periodic checkpoint of region state for truncation.",
+        "required_fields": ["region_id", "epoch", "active_task_count", "pending_obligation_count", "sequence_number"]
+      },
+      {
+        "entry_type": "JE-CRASH-MARKER",
+        "description": "Crash event written at the point of failure.",
+        "required_fields": ["region_id", "crash_reason", "epoch", "sequence_number", "last_checkpoint_seq"]
+      },
+      {
+        "entry_type": "JE-RECOVERY-COMPLETE",
+        "description": "Recovery from journal replay completed.",
+        "required_fields": ["region_id", "replayed_entries", "recovered_tasks", "abandoned_obligations", "epoch", "sequence_number"]
+      }
+    ],
+    "ordering_rules": [
+      {
+        "rule_id": "JO-MONOTONIC-SEQ",
+        "description": "Sequence numbers within a region are strictly monotonically increasing."
+      },
+      {
+        "rule_id": "JO-EPOCH-MONOTONIC",
+        "description": "Epoch values are non-decreasing within a journal."
+      },
+      {
+        "rule_id": "JO-OPEN-BEFORE-SPAWN",
+        "description": "JE-REGION-OPEN must precede any JE-TASK-SPAWN for the same region."
+      },
+      {
+        "rule_id": "JO-ENTER-BEFORE-SETTLE",
+        "description": "JE-OBLIGATION-ENTER must precede JE-OBLIGATION-SETTLE for the same obligation."
+      },
+      {
+        "rule_id": "JO-CHECKPOINT-TRUNCATABLE",
+        "description": "Entries before the last checkpoint may be truncated without losing recovery capability."
+      }
+    ]
+  },
+  "microreboot_protocol": {
+    "description": "Protocol for restarting a crashed region from its journal without full runtime restart.",
+    "phases": [
+      {
+        "phase_id": "MR-ISOLATE",
+        "description": "Fence the crashed region: cancel in-flight I/O, detach from parent scheduler.",
+        "precondition": "Region is in JOURNALED state.",
+        "postcondition": "All region resources are quiesced and no external references remain."
+      },
+      {
+        "phase_id": "MR-REPLAY",
+        "description": "Replay journal from last checkpoint, reconstructing region state.",
+        "precondition": "MR-ISOLATE complete. Journal is valid and readable.",
+        "postcondition": "Region state matches the last consistent checkpoint."
+      },
+      {
+        "phase_id": "MR-RECONCILE",
+        "description": "Reconcile obligations: re-enter pending obligations, abandon those that cannot be recovered.",
+        "precondition": "MR-REPLAY complete.",
+        "postcondition": "Obligation table reflects recovered state. Abandoned obligations are logged."
+      },
+      {
+        "phase_id": "MR-RESUME",
+        "description": "Transition region to RUNNING, re-register with parent scheduler.",
+        "precondition": "MR-RECONCILE complete. Supervision policy allows restart.",
+        "postcondition": "Region is RUNNING and accepting new tasks."
+      }
+    ],
+    "budget": {
+      "max_replay_entries": 10000,
+      "max_recovery_wall_clock_ms": 5000,
+      "max_consecutive_microreboots": 3,
+      "backoff_base_ms": 100,
+      "backoff_multiplier": 2.0,
+      "backoff_max_ms": 10000
+    },
+    "cancellation_interaction": {
+      "description": "How microreboot interacts with structured cancellation.",
+      "rules": [
+        {
+          "rule_id": "CR-CANCEL-FENCE",
+          "description": "Cancellation tokens for the crashed region are fenced: cancel signals are suppressed until MR-RESUME."
+        },
+        {
+          "rule_id": "CR-PARENT-PROPAGATE",
+          "description": "If the parent region cancels during microreboot, the recovery is aborted and the region is TOMBSTONED."
+        },
+        {
+          "rule_id": "CR-CHILD-ABANDON",
+          "description": "Child regions of a crashing region receive immediate cancellation and are TOMBSTONED."
+        }
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "supervision_mapping": {
+    "description": "How existing supervision strategies map to crash-only semantics.",
+    "mappings": [
+      {
+        "strategy": "Stop",
+        "crash_only_behavior": "CRASHING -> JOURNALED -> TOMBSTONED. No recovery attempted.",
+        "journal_action": "Write crash marker, no replay."
+      },
+      {
+        "strategy": "Restart",
+        "crash_only_behavior": "CRASHING -> JOURNALED -> RECOVERING -> RUNNING (up to budget).",
+        "journal_action": "Write crash marker, attempt journal replay with backoff."
+      },
+      {
+        "strategy": "Escalate",
+        "crash_only_behavior": "CRASHING -> JOURNALED -> TOMBSTONED. Parent region notified for escalation.",
+        "journal_action": "Write crash marker, propagate failure to parent supervisor."
+      }
+    ]
+  },
+  "structured_log_fields_required": [
+    "region_id",
+    "journal_entry_type",
+    "sequence_number",
+    "epoch",
+    "crash_state",
+    "crash_reason",
+    "recovery_phase",
+    "replay_entries_count",
+    "abandoned_obligations",
+    "recovered_tasks",
+    "microreboot_attempt",
+    "wall_clock_ms",
+    "supervision_strategy",
+    "parent_region_id"
+  ],
+  "smoke_scenarios": [
+    {
+      "scenario_id": "COR-SMOKE-STATE-MACHINE",
+      "description": "Validate crash state machine transitions and invariants",
+      "command": "rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-codex-aa091 cargo test --test crash_only_region_contract state -- --nocapture"
+    },
+    {
+      "scenario_id": "COR-SMOKE-JOURNAL",
+      "description": "Validate journal format, entry types, and ordering rules",
+      "command": "rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-codex-aa091 cargo test --test crash_only_region_contract journal -- --nocapture"
+    },
+    {
+      "scenario_id": "COR-SMOKE-MICROREBOOT",
+      "description": "Validate microreboot protocol phases, budget, and cancellation interaction",
+      "command": "rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-codex-aa091 cargo test --test crash_only_region_contract microreboot -- --nocapture"
+    }
+  ],

+}

@@ -0,0 +1,103 @@
+# Crash-Only Region Semantics Contract
+
+Bead: `asupersync-1508v.9.4`
+
+## Purpose
+
+This contract defines crash-only execution semantics for supervised regions: explicit state machines for crash and recovery, a deterministic journal format for replay, and a microreboot protocol that interacts cleanly with structured cancellation and obligation settlement.
+
+## Contract Artifacts
+
+1. Canonical artifact: `artifacts/crash_only_region_semantics_v1.json`
+2. Smoke runner: `scripts/run_crash_only_region_smoke.sh`
+3. Invariant suite: `tests/crash_only_region_contract.rs`
+
+## Crash State Machine
+
+Regions follow a strict state machine: RUNNING -> DRAINING or CRASHING -> JOURNALED -> RECOVERING or TOMBSTONED.
+
+| State | Description |
+|-------|------------|
+| RUNNING | Actively executing tasks and settling obligations |
+| DRAINING | Graceful shutdown: no new tasks, existing run to completion |
+| CRASHING | Unrecoverable fault detected, journal checkpoint being written |
+| JOURNALED | Crash journal is durable, state frozen for replay |
+| RECOVERING | Microreboot in progress, replaying journal |
+| QUIESCED | Graceful drain complete, all obligations settled |
+| TOMBSTONED | Terminal: resources released, no revival |
+
+### State Machine Invariants
+
+- **CSM-NO-SKIP**: No transition may skip intermediate states
+- **CSM-TOMBSTONE-TERMINAL**: TOMBSTONED is absorbing
+- **CSM-CRASH-ALWAYS-JOURNALS**: CRASHING must reach JOURNALED before any other state
+- **CSM-RECOVER-NEEDS-JOURNAL**: RECOVERING requires a valid JOURNALED predecessor
+
+## Journal Format
+
+Journals are deterministic, sequenced logs sufficient for crash recovery.
+
+### Entry Types
+
+| Entry | Purpose |
+|-------|---------|
+| JE-REGION-OPEN | Region opened with configuration |
+| JE-TASK-SPAWN | Task spawned in region |
+| JE-OBLIGATION-ENTER | Obligation entered |
+| JE-OBLIGATION-SETTLE | Obligation settled (fulfilled or abandoned) |
+| JE-DECISION-APPLIED | Controller decision applied |
+| JE-CHECKPOINT | Periodic state checkpoint for truncation |
+| JE-CRASH-MARKER | Crash event at point of failure |
+| JE-RECOVERY-COMPLETE | Recovery replay finished |
+
+### Ordering Rules
+
+- **JO-MONOTONIC-SEQ**: Sequence numbers strictly increasing within a region
+- **JO-EPOCH-MONOTONIC**: Epoch values non-decreasing
+- **JO-OPEN-BEFORE-SPAWN**: Region open precedes task spawn
+- **JO-ENTER-BEFORE-SETTLE**: Obligation enter precedes settle
+- **JO-CHECKPOINT-TRUNCATABLE**: Pre-checkpoint entries may be truncated
+
+## Microreboot Protocol
+
+Four-phase protocol for restarting a crashed region without full runtime restart:
+
+1. **MR-ISOLATE**: Fence the crashed region, cancel in-flight I/O
+2. **MR-REPLAY**: Replay journal from last checkpoint
+3. **MR-RECONCILE**: Re-enter pending obligations, abandon unrecoverable ones
+4. **MR-RESUME**: Transition to RUNNING, re-register with scheduler
+
+### Budget Constraints
+
+- Max replay entries: 10,000
+- Max recovery wall clock: 5,000ms
+- Max consecutive microreboots: 3
+- Exponential backoff: 100ms base, 2x multiplier, 10s cap
+
+## Cancellation Interaction
+
+- **CR-CANCEL-FENCE**: Cancel signals suppressed during microreboot until MR-RESUME
+- **CR-PARENT-PROPAGATE**: Parent cancel during recovery aborts to TOMBSTONED
+- **CR-CHILD-ABANDON**: Child regions of crashed region receive immediate cancellation
+
+## Supervision Mapping
+
+| Strategy | Crash-Only Behavior |
+|----------|-------------------|
+| Stop | CRASHING -> JOURNALED -> TOMBSTONED |
+| Restart | CRASHING -> JOURNALED -> RECOVERING -> RUNNING |
+| Escalate | CRASHING -> JOURNALED -> TOMBSTONED + parent notification |
+
+## Validation
+
+```bash
+rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-codex-aa091 cargo test --test crash_only_region_contract -- --nocapture
+```
+
+## Cross-References
+
+- `artifacts/crash_only_region_semantics_v1.json`
+- `src/supervision.rs` -- SupervisionStrategy, RestartConfig
+- `src/runtime/kernel.rs` -- RecoveryCommand, ControllerRegistry
+- `src/trace/crashpack.rs` -- CrashPack deterministic repro format
+- `src/signal/shutdown.rs` -- ShutdownController

@@ -0,0 +1,136 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+set -euo pipefail
+
+# Schema anchors for contract invariants:
+# - crash-only-region-smoke-bundle-v1
+# - crash-only-region-smoke-run-report-v1
+
+SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
+PROJECT_ROOT="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"
+CONTRACT_ARTIFACT="${PROJECT_ROOT}/artifacts/crash_only_region_semantics_v1.json"
+OUTPUT_ROOT="${COR_SMOKE_OUTPUT_DIR:-${PROJECT_ROOT}/target/crash-only-region-smoke}"
+TIMESTAMP="$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)"
+RUN_DIR="${OUTPUT_ROOT}/run_${TIMESTAMP}"
+LIST_ONLY=0
+DRY_RUN=1
+
+declare -a SELECTED_SCENARIOS=()
+
+usage() {
+    cat <<'USAGE'
+Usage: ./scripts/run_crash_only_region_smoke.sh [options]
+
+Options:
+  --list                    List scenario IDs and exit
+  --scenario <id>           Run one scenario (repeatable)
+  --output-root <dir>       Override output root
+  --dry-run                 Emit manifests without executing (default)
+  --execute                 Execute cargo test scenarios
+  -h, --help                Show help
+USAGE
+}
+
+require_tools() {
+    if ! command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+        echo "FATAL: jq is required" >&2; exit 1
+    fi
+    if [ ! -f "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT" ]; then
+        echo "FATAL: contract artifact missing at ${CONTRACT_ARTIFACT}" >&2; exit 1
+    fi
+}
+
+json_escape() { printf '%s' "$1" | sed 's/\\/\\\\/g; s/"/\\"/g'; }
+contract_version() { jq -r '.contract_version' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT"; }
+bundle_schema_version() { jq -r '.runner_bundle_schema_version' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT"; }
+report_schema_version() { jq -r '.runner_report_schema_version' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT"; }
+
+list_scenarios() {
+    jq -r '.smoke_scenarios[] | [.scenario_id, .description] | @tsv' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT" \
+        | while IFS=$'\t' read -r sid desc; do printf '%-38s %s\n' "$sid" "$desc"; done
+}
+
+load_scenario_json() {
+    jq -c --arg sid "$1" '.smoke_scenarios[] | select(.scenario_id == $sid)' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT"
+}
+
+RESULTS_JSON=""
+append_result() {
+    if [[ -z "$RESULTS_JSON" ]]; then RESULTS_JSON="$1"; else RESULTS_JSON="${RESULTS_JSON},$1"; fi
+}
+
+run_scenario() {
+    local sid="$1" scenario_json description command scenario_dir log_file summary_file started_ts ended_ts status rc
+    scenario_json="$(load_scenario_json "$sid")"
+    [[ -z "$scenario_json" ]] && { echo "FATAL: unknown scenario: $sid" >&2; return 1; }
+    description="$(jq -r '.description' <<<"$scenario_json")"
+    command="$(jq -r '.command' <<<"$scenario_json")"
+    scenario_dir="${RUN_DIR}/${sid}"; log_file="${scenario_dir}/run.log"; summary_file="${scenario_dir}/bundle_manifest.json"
+    mkdir -p "$scenario_dir"
+    started_ts="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
+    echo ">>> Running scenario ${sid}"
+    if [[ "$DRY_RUN" -eq 1 ]]; then
+        printf 'DRY_RUN scenario=%s\n' "$sid" >"$log_file"; rc=0; status="dry_run"
+    else
+        rc=0; eval "$command" >"$log_file" 2>&1 || rc=$?
+        status="$( [[ "$rc" -eq 0 ]] && printf "passed" || printf "failed" )"
+    fi
+    ended_ts="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
+    cat >"$summary_file" <<JSON
+{
+  "schema_version": "$(json_escape "$(bundle_schema_version)")",
+  "contract_version": "$(json_escape "$(contract_version)")",
+  "scenario_id": "$(json_escape "$sid")",
+  "description": "$(json_escape "$description")",
+  "status": "$(json_escape "$status")",
+  "exit_code": ${rc},
+  "started_ts": "$(json_escape "$started_ts")",
+  "ended_ts": "$(json_escape "$ended_ts")"
+}
+JSON
+    append_result "$(jq -c '.' "$summary_file")"
+    [[ "$rc" -eq 0 ]]
+}
+
+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+    case "$1" in
+        --list) LIST_ONLY=1; shift ;;
+        --scenario) SELECTED_SCENARIOS+=("${2:-}"); shift 2 ;;
+        --output-root) OUTPUT_ROOT="${2:-}"; RUN_DIR="${OUTPUT_ROOT}/run_${TIMESTAMP}"; shift 2 ;;
+        --dry-run) DRY_RUN=1; shift ;;
+        --execute) DRY_RUN=0; shift ;;
+        -h|--help) usage; exit 0 ;;
+        *) echo "Unknown: $1" >&2; usage >&2; exit 1 ;;
+    esac
+done
+
+require_tools
+if [[ "$LIST_ONLY" -eq 1 ]]; then list_scenarios; exit 0; fi
+if [[ "${#SELECTED_SCENARIOS[@]}" -eq 0 ]]; then
+    mapfile -t SELECTED_SCENARIOS < <(jq -r '.smoke_scenarios[].scenario_id' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT")
+fi
+
+mkdir -p "$RUN_DIR"; OVERALL_RC=0
+for sid in "${SELECTED_SCENARIOS[@]}"; do run_scenario "$sid" || OVERALL_RC=1; done
+
+RUN_REPORT="${RUN_DIR}/run_report.json"
+cat >"$RUN_REPORT" <<JSON
+{
+  "schema_version": "$(json_escape "$(report_schema_version)")",
+  "contract_version": "$(json_escape "$(contract_version)")",
+  "run_dir": "$(json_escape "$RUN_DIR")",
+  "dry_run": $( [[ "$DRY_RUN" -eq 1 ]] && printf 'true' || printf 'false' ),
+  "results": [${RESULTS_JSON}],
+  "status": "$([ "$OVERALL_RC" -eq 0 ] && printf "passed" || printf "failed")"
+}
+JSON
+
+echo ""
+echo "==================================================================="
+echo "       CRASH-ONLY REGION SMOKE SUMMARY                             "
+echo "==================================================================="
+echo "  Run dir:   ${RUN_DIR}"
+echo "  Mode:      $([ "$DRY_RUN" -eq 1 ] && printf "DRY-RUN" || printf "EXECUTE")"
+echo "  Status:    $([ "$OVERALL_RC" -eq 0 ] && printf "PASSED" || printf "FAILED")"
+echo "==================================================================="
+
+exit "$OVERALL_RC"
